body.title: agent_datacenter: rack.health on-demand fan-out across all registered devices
body.result: health.py rack_health_async(): asyncio.gather + to_thread per device, timeout=5s default. rack_health_sync() for non-async callers. Timeout → {healthy:False, error:'timeout'}.
body.description:
Implement rack.health as an on-demand polling call — not a heartbeat loop. When queried, the rack goes and checks each registered device in parallel.

Design decision: no background heartbeat. Health is a question, not a process. Poll when you need the answer; callers decide how often.

Proposed shape:
- In health.py: async def rack_health() using asyncio.gather()
  For each registered (non-offline) device: call device.health() with timeout=5s (configurable)
  Aggregate: {device_id: {healthy: bool, details: dict, error: str|None, block_type: 'auto'|'manual'|None}}
  Devices that time out: {healthy: False, error: 'timeout', ...}
  Devices that raise: {healthy: False, error: str(exception), ...}

Affected files:
- ~/dev/src/agent_datacenter/agent_datacenter/skeleton/health.py (rack_health implementation)

Design rules:
- No background threads — asyncio.gather with timeout, not a daemon
- docs-in-code: comment explaining why no heartbeat (poll-on-demand rationale from spec §Health)

Scope boundary:
IN: parallel health fan-out, timeout handling, error capture per device
OUT: per-device health implementations (those live in each device's device.py)

Test plan:
- rack.health() with 3 stub devices: 1 healthy, 1 timeout, 1 error → correct result shape
- Healthy device: {healthy: True, details: {...}, error: None}
- Timeout device: {healthy: False, error: 'timeout'}
- Error device: {healthy: False, error: 'ConnectionRefused...'}
